svg.text: Software Overview Diagram - Robotics Challenge Robot
Arduino GIGA R1 WiFi control software: sensing -> state estimation -> mission decisions -> control -> actuators
Includes pin map, I2C addresses, route strategies, line-loss policy, planting flow, and safety overrides
- Default line threshold: th = 650; tune in Serial
- Primary sensor when driving on solid black grid
- I2C Wire bus: D20 SDA / D21 SCL
- Detects grid node / hole tag UID
- I2C Wire bus: D20 SDA / D21 SCL
- Startup gyro bias calibration; yaw = 0 at initial
- Used for absolute turn targets: -90 deg then back to
- C1 rising-edge interrupt; C2 gives direction
- Used for distance, offset-to-hole, and encoder-
- Echo uses level shifting to 3.3 V for GIGA safety
- Used for wall following and advanced obstacle checks
- Mechanical kill button: D32 -> switch -> GND,
- Revival button: D31 -> switch -> GND, INPUT_PULLUP
- LDR voltage divider: A0, with 10 kOhm fixed resistor
- Modulino Distance sensor: I2C 0x29, above planting
- All sensor, servo, motor, and battery grounds share
- Server can send teacher stop / enable commands
- Robot can query all 81 node states at startup
- Robot reports planted nodes and receives rescue
- Read RC discharge time for each detector
- Compute qtrNorm[0..8] and weighted line position
- active / inactive detector counts are printed for
- Normal: enough detectors see black line -> line
- If 8/9 detectors are below threshold: line is
- In solid-grid test: drive straight instead of
- Latest requested behavior: line-lost straight uses
- Poll RFID periodically without blocking main loop
- Convert UID bytes to readable tag ID
- In trial run 2: first detected RFID plants
- In full strategy: UID maps to grid node, then
- Encoder deltas are converted to wheel distance
- distance_mm -> target counts using wheel diameter
- For straight sections, left/right encoder count
- Integrate yaw and normalize to -180..180 deg
- Do not reset yaw before each turn; startup yaw
- Measure echo pulse width -> distance in mm/cm
- Wall following uses left or right sonar depending
- Advanced obstacle mode: if front object stays
after confirmation wait, mark cell blocked
- Stores 9 x 9 grid state from server: fertilized /
- Can preselect target holes before driving
- If another robot plants a chosen node, route
- Simple / Trial Run 2: follow solid line, plant fixed 5
- Medium demonstrations: fixed trajectories, saving routine,
WiFi kill, servo, motor, sensor tests
- Advanced / final: 9 x 9 map planning, rescue tasks,
obstacle confirmation, BFS replanning, emergency return
- Marked area: prefer QTR line following; unmarked area:
- INIT: start Serial, Wire/Wire1, Motoron, RFID, QTR, servo,
- FOLLOW_LINE or WALL_FOLLOW: drive toward next node
- RFID_DETECTED: stop, read UID, decide whether to plant /
- DRIVE_OFFSET: ignore line, move fixed distance so seed
- PLANT: servo advances 60 deg, LED / Serial status updates
- TURN_SCHEDULED: IMU absolute yaw turn; COMPLETE / STOPPED
- Trial run 2: plant at every detected RFID until 5 seeds are
- Full route: ask server whether node is fertilized and not
- If plantable: drive offset, drop seed, record LDR
- If not plantable: continue route or replan if target is no
- Optional Modulino Distance check validates seed hole
- If QTR line is visible: use line-follow PID
- If 8/9 QTR detectors miss black line: stop trusting line
- In solid-grid task: use encoder-balanced straight drive
- In open-field / wall-follow tasks: switch to sonar wall
following with target distance based on grid row/column
- Server rescue request stores one or more rescue coordinates
- Robot continues mission until it reaches the rescue column
- Slow approach after RFID; wait for revival button or two
- Reverse to previous RFID, drive 7.5 cm forward to center on
hole, then IMU turn 180 deg and resume
- Mechanical kill: first press/release stops; second
- WiFi kill: pause; re-enable can trigger emergency-return
- Advanced obstacle: front sonar object -> wait/confirm ->
mark next cell blocked -> BFS around it
- If remaining match time is low: stop planting and return to
- Input: lineError from QTR weighted position
- L = base + correction; R = base - correction
- Tunable through Serial: linep, linei, lined,
- Input: target mm and encoder counts
- Uses average of left/right absolute counts to
- Uses count difference to keep straight when line
- Used for planting offset and straight dead-
- Input: target absolute yaw, not relative reset
- First trial turn: target yaw = -90 deg
- Second trial turn: target yaw = 0 deg, returning
- Program command is strictly equal magnitude and
- Used when operating without solid black lines
- Input: left or right sonar distance to wall
- Target distance depends on arena edge and grid
- Optional trigonometric correction can use sonar
- Simple/medium demos can disable full obstacle
- Full map is 9 x 9: rows A-I, columns 1-9
- Start / exit can be C9 -> G9 depending challenge
- Choose plant targets by score, closeness to
wall, row grouping, and low turn count
- Replan only on: new rescue target, confirmed
obstacle, emergency return, or selected seed
- Highest priority: kill / WiFi stop -> motors = 0
- Next: obstacle or rescue waiting state
- Normal mission decisions only run when safety is
- All long actions call missionAbortRequested() so
stop works during servo, turn, and distance
- M1 = left track, M2 = right track
- Motor VIN from external battery / shield
- Encoder VCC to 3.3 V, GND common
- Startup reset to 0 deg; each seed drop
- Prints RFID UID, encoder counts, yaw,
- Register robot name and team ID through
- Query 81-hole map states at startup or
information when teacher API is available
How the Robot Chooses What to Do
- Marked solid grid: QTR line sensor is primary. Follow line using line PID while RFID tags
- No-line / open-field: if 8 or more QTR detectors do not see black, stop trusting QTR.
- In solid-grid trial, line-loss falls back to encoder-balanced straight drive; in open-
field tasks it switches to sonar wall following or dead reckoning.
- Simple and medium demos intentionally avoid full dynamic obstacle handling so the
- Full navigation uses server map + BFS. Obstacles are only added to the robot map after
front sonar confirms they remain after a waiting period.
- Replanning is limited to important events: rescue request, confirmed obstacle, emergency
return, or selected target seed status changed.
- RFID tag is detected at a grid node or hole approach point.
- Trial run 2: immediately drive kPlantingOffsetMm and plant until 5 seeds are
- Full run: query cached/server state. Only plant if fertilized = true and planted =
- Drive offset using encoder distance; optional Modulino Distance checks chute-to-hole
- Servo advances 60 deg to release one seed.
- Robot records LDR brightness and reports planted to server when the API command is
- Mechanical kill D32 has highest local priority. First press/release stops; second
press/release resets, recalibrates IMU, and restarts the route.
- WiFi teacher kill can pause the robot through MiniMessenger. In final logic, re-enable can
trigger emergency return instead of simply continuing.
- Long operations such as servo hold, distance drive, pause, and IMU turn all check
- Serial Monitor shows mode, active/inactive QTR count, encoder correction, yaw, target yaw,
UID, seed count, and state so failures can be diagnosed live.
Kill / WiFi stop can interrupt any state
Blue = hardware inputs Teal = signal processing Purple = mission logic Orange = control algorithms Green = actuators / outputs Red dashed = safety override
Generated as vector SVG: text remains selectable and crisp on GitHub
